platform.name: Arduino
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: The developer has a supported computer and permission to install software.
An Arduino board and a compatible data USB cable are available for a hardware upload outcome.
No Arduino IDE install, board package, Cloud Editor session, or Arduino account is assumed yet.

developer_goal: Reach Arduino's official first sketch-running-on-board success from a fresh developer setup, including account creation only if the selected route requires it.

first_success.outcome: A sketch is compiled, uploaded, and starts running on the connected Arduino board.
first_success.observable_signal: Upload completes without error and the sketch starts on the board; with the guide's Blink example, LED_BUILTIN behavior is visible on boards that expose it.

route_selection.surface: Official Arduino IDE sketch upload path versus Arduino Cloud Editor
route_selection.rule: Begin at the account-creation boundary required by the corpus policy, then select the shortest complete self-service route to a running sketch. Do not invent a winner when the documented primary IDE path omits account creation.

candidates.name: Desktop Arduino IDE 2 upload of built-in Blink
candidates.reason: Official Help Center upload guide and software page route new developers here and end with the sketch running on the board, but the path does not create an Arduino account, which conflicts with the corpus account-creation boundary.

candidates.name: Arduino Cloud Editor with Create Agent and account
candidates.reason: Requires Arduino account signup, email verification, Create Agent install, and browser upload; reaches a similar board-programmed result on a different surface.

candidates.name: Arduino CLI upload
candidates.reason: Documented as an alternate tooling surface; the docs home and first-upload guide select the graphical IDE route.

required_path.action: Create an Arduino account with the email route if the selected surface is Cloud Editor or another account-gated Arduino service.

required_fields.label: Privacy Policy and Terms of Service acceptance
Date of birth
required_fields.notes: Entered as DD/MM/YYYY after the initial credentials step.

required_path.observable_result: Arduino accepts the signup details and sends an email verification message.

required_path.action: Open the verification email and select Confirm Now.
required_path.observable_result: The Arduino account registration is complete.

required_path.action: Download and install the current Arduino IDE 2 release for the computer's operating system if the selected route is the desktop IDE.
Connect the Arduino board to the computer with a compatible data USB cable.
required_path.observable_result: The board powers on and is available for IDE detection.

required_path.action: Install the board package when Arduino IDE reports that support files for the detected board are missing.
required_path.observable_result: The board can be selected without a missing-platform warning.

required_path.action: Select the correct board and serial port using the IDE 2 board selector or Tools menu.
required_path.observable_result: Arduino IDE is configured for the connected board and upload port.

required_path.action: Open the built-in Blink example through File > Examples > 01.Basics > Blink.
required_path.observable_result: The Blink sketch is loaded and ready to compile.

required_path.action: Click Upload and wait while the IDE compiles and programs the board.
required_path.observable_result: The upload finishes without a failed-upload error.

required_path.action: Observe the uploaded sketch starting on the board.
required_path.observable_result: The sketch runs on the hardware; for Blink on boards with LED_BUILTIN, the onboard LED changes state repeatedly.

prerequisites.description: A supported Windows, macOS, or 64-bit Linux computer for Arduino IDE 2.
An Arduino board and a compatible data USB cable, not a charge-only cable.
Network access to download the IDE and any missing board package.

external_gates.description: Operating-system permission for application install and USB or serial-port access may be required.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email delivery and confirmation are required to finish Arduino account signup when the Cloud route is used.
Board-package download, compilation, and upload must finish before the sketch can run on the board.

platform_outcomes.description: Arduino creates the account after email verification when the account route is used.
Arduino IDE compiles the sketch and transfers it to board flash memory.
The uploaded sketch starts running on the board and runs again after reset.

excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a measured product action.
The upload guide treats Verify as optional because Upload compiles.

excluded.item: Arduino CLI and App Lab first runs
excluded.reason: Different surfaces from the Help Center IDE upload guide.

sources.title: Upload a sketch in Arduino IDE
If your board is not detected by Arduino IDE
Blink built-in example context in upload guide
Arduino login
evidence_supported: Public login and account entry surface

uncertainties.question: Does the corpus account-creation boundary require selecting Arduino Cloud Editor even though the official first-upload guide uses desktop IDE without an account?
uncertainties.impact: Choosing Cloud adds account, verification, and Create Agent steps; choosing IDE omits account creation and cannot satisfy the boundary as written.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A maintained policy decision that either exempts hardware IDE-only first success from account creation or designates Cloud Editor as the measured Arduino route.

uncertainties.question: For a given board family, is the automatic board-package prompt always required, and is LED_BUILTIN Blink behavior always the observable terminal?
uncertainties.impact: Package install and hardware verification steps change by board and may drop out of the required path.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded board model plus a dated walkthrough of IDE package prompts and onboard LED behavior for that model.
